Just now

--

Photo by Jakub Żerdzicki on UnsplashA $20-a-month AI app looks like the cleanest margin in software: no inventory, no shipping, code that copies itself for free. Model it honestly and the take-home on that $20 subscription lands under $5 — and at the scale most of these apps actually reach, under zero.
The line everyone fixates on — the model API bill — is the smallest movable number on the page. On a heavy month, calling Claude Haiku 4.5 costs about $1.20 per user (Anthropic pricing: $1 per million input tokens, $5 per million output). That’s roughly 6% of revenue. Founders lose sleep over the 6% and ignore the lines that actually decide whether this is a business. Here’s the whole page.
The $20 you charge is not the $20 you keep
Before a single token is spent, the payment rail takes its cut. Stripe’s US standard rate is 2.9% + $0.30 per online card charge (Stripe pricing). On a $20 charge that’s $0.88 — and the flat $0.30 is the part that stings at low prices. Run that same formula on a $5 charge and you pay $0.44–8.8%. Small tickets are where the fixed fee bites hardest, and $20/mo is a small ticket.
